title: bassia littorea
intro: Bassia littorea, also known as littoral Bassia or beach saltwort, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. It is a halophytic plant, adapted to thrive in coastal areas with high salt concentrations. The leaves and stems of Bassia littorea are rich in bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which have been studied for their potential medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations of Bassia littorea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
